key_insight: A DSL-guided, symbolic-predicate loop can make LLM agents more reliable for inverse materials design by grounding natural-language rules in statistical evidence.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to design candidate perovskite oxides for target properties without requiring large task-specific training datasets or retraining.
method: Translate natural-language design rules into predicates in a chemistry DSL.
Use accumulated materials data to test, validate, and refine rules during iterative agent search.
Deploy a multi-agent framework (ORCHESTRA) for inverse design of double perovskite oxides.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that symbolic predicates helped identify unsupported rules, validate newly proposed rules, and improve the rule store over iterative design cycles, with better performance than natural-language-only rules, especially for harder target properties.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the underlying materials data and DSL predicates, so weak statistical grounding could mislead rule refinement.
paper_title: Symbolic Predicate-Guided Language Agents for Inverse Design of Perovskite Oxides
